new_production_code_detail: engine/catalog/recovery.go (NEW FILE): RecoverFromWAL(cat *Catalog, walDir string) error. Not pure test scaffolding -- flagged clearly per instructions. Closes a gap explicitly pre-documented in catalog.go's Catalog doc comment (empty index on load) using the already-verified wal.Replay entrypoint (engine/wal, issue #3). No changes to engine/wal itself.
verification_scope_notes: Verify RecoverFromWAL's correctness/completeness as a general-purpose reconstruction mechanism, not just that this one test passes -- it re-applies CatalogPut/CatalogDelete via existing Catalog.Put/Delete (delete-then-reinsert semantics on an initially-empty index), and silently skips BTree record types (by design, scoped to catalog reconstruction only) and tolerates ErrNotFound on replayed deletes (by design, defensive).
Confirm no engine/wal changes were needed (architecture-discovery.md documents why: wal.Replay's existing API/semantics fully sufficed).
Confirm test doesn't leak goroutines/fds across the two generations (gen1 handles explicitly closed before gen2 opens, verified via -race clean run).
Check whether closing issue #4 is warranted once this passes verification -- this was flagged as the last subtask.
self_consistency_summary: gofmt/go vet/go build clean; go test ./catalog/... -run TestContentDurabilityRestart -race -v -count=1 PASS; full engine/catalog package -race -v -count=1 PASS (no regressions on 1.4.1/1.4.2/1.4.3); go test ./... (whole engine module) PASS.
